SEARCH
Package org.javalabs.jpa.query

Interface Expr

All Superinterfaces:
Visitable
All Known Implementing Classes:
QueryExpr
public interface Expr extends Visitable
Represents a criteria expression.

An Expr object represents a primitive expression in which a single criteria is applied to a target and a set of arguments to return a result. An Expr has three components:

  • L.H.S - Left operand, which is mostly the name of the table column
  • Operator
  • R.H.S - Right operand, which is the value for the column
Followings are the example of some valid expressions:
  • location = 'NY'
  • salary > 35000
  • age <= 50
  • age BETWEEN 18 AND 40
  • department IN ('HR', 'RAndD', 'Finance')
Author:
Sudiptasish Chanda

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String
    lhs()
    Return the left side operand of this expression.
    Operator
    ops()
    Return the operator.
    Object
    rhs()
    Return the right side operand of this expression.

    accept

  • Method Details

    • lhs

      String lhs()
      Return the left side operand of this expression.
      Returns:
      String

    • ops

      Operator ops()
      Return the operator.
      Returns:
      Operator

    • rhs

      Object rhs()
      Return the right side operand of this expression. It will be null for ISNULL or ISNOTNULL operator. For BETWEEN operator, it will have two values.
      Returns:
      String
